title: The Thiele Machine: A Computational Model with Explicit Structural Cost
description: The Thiele Machine is a computational model in which a transition from "unverified" to "verified" cannot occur unless it pays a positive structural cost - a rule (A2) enforced in the step relation rather than in checkable software. It computes exactly the same functions as a Turing machine, adding one axis of state and one constraint on how a result may be claimed; because a classical machine has no state slot to carry that constraint at the step-transition level, it is a structurally lossy projection of a substrate that does. The repository contains the machine-checked Coq kernel (zero project-local axioms; zero admits across 267 files, 3,823 theorems probed), an OCaml runner extracted from it, a Kami/Bluespec/Verilog RTL realization, and the accompanying monograph. The software is licensed Apache-2.0; the monograph and distillation are licensed CC-BY-SA-4.0.
